atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -0,0 +1,191 @@; block-build trace types, env config, and ring-buffer setup
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds the block-build attempt trace data model, environment-driven enable/capacity configuration, capped ring-buffer globals, timestamp helper, bool parser, and read helpers for trace enablement, capacity, and retained attempt count.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 1-191 exactly match source-added diff lines 54960-55150.
Ritual RPC reads recent block-build attempts for debug/inspection responses.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact preservation keeps the trace schema, environment defaults, maximum capacity clamp, poison-lock recovery, and ring-buffer initialization semantics unchanged.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: An invalid or zero `RITUAL_BLOCK_BUILD_TRACE_CAPACITY` should fall back to the default, while an excessive value should clamp to `MAX_BLOCK_BUILD_TRACE_CAPACITY`.

atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -0,0 +193,245 @@; record and read recent block-build attempts
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence records each payload build attempt into the capped in-memory ring buffer when tracing is enabled, assigns monotonic local sequence numbers, evicts the oldest attempt at capacity, and exposes newest-first snapshots bounded by configured capacity.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 193-245 exactly match source-added diff lines 55152-55204.
Payload builder records attempt outcome, phase timings, demotions, cap observations, primary cause, and included transaction count when the recorder drops.
Ritual RPC uses `recent_block_build_attempts(limit)` to expose the retained attempt trace.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact mapping preserves trace enablement gating, sequence assignment, FIFO eviction, newest-first snapshot order, and lock-poison recovery behavior.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: Recording more attempts than capacity should evict the oldest attempts and return newest-first results from `recent_block_build_attempts`.

atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -0,0 +247,321 @@; common timestamp/path helpers and generic phase logging
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds common block lifecycle logging helpers: microsecond timestamps, saturating duration-to-ms conversion, phase label to path inference for payload/block-accept/canonical-revival/lifecycle categories, and generic structured debug logging with optional reason.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 247-321 exactly match source-added diff lines 55206-55280.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact preservation keeps log labels, target, path inference, duration calculation, optional reason handling, and structured field names unchanged.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: A `canonical_revival_*` phase should be logged with path `canonical_revival`, while unknown labels should fall back to `block_lifecycle`.

atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds structured block verification lifecycle logs for start, failure, and success, including block hash/number, verification timestamps, error variant extraction from `Debug`, full display reason, gas usage, gas limit, block size, base fee, and block timestamp.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 323-429 exactly match source-added diff lines 55282-55388.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact mapping preserves the block verification event labels, outcome fields, error classification method, and block metadata fields.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: A verification error whose `Debug` string contains tuple fields should log only the variant name before `(` while preserving the full display string as `reason`.

atoms.source_hunk_hint: @@ -0,0 +431,562 @@; payload build start/end and per-phase attempt logging
atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds payload-building lifecycle logs for start/end and per-phase timings, tagging payload id, block time/number, attempt id/kind, stage, outcome, block hash, gas used, duration, and transaction count.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 431-562 exactly match source-added diff lines 55390-55521.
The Ethereum payload builder calls these metrics helpers around payload build attempts and phases.
Unified execution code also logs attempt-tagged payload phases through the preserved helper.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact preservation keeps the payload-build observability contract, stage/outcome labels, attempt metadata, and per-phase duration/count fields unchanged.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: A payload build start with `block_number: None` should omit the block number field while still logging payload id, block time, attempt id, and attempt kind.

atoms.semantic_claim: The old divergence adds a structured execution-layer head update log event carrying timestamp, canonical head block hash, block number, and block time under the block lifecycle target.

destinations.evidence: Destination lines 564-587 exactly match source-added diff lines 55523-55546.
Engine tree calls `ritual_metrics::block::log_el_update_head` when updating the canonical tip.

atoms.equivalence_argument: Exact mapping preserves canonical-head update observability fields and labels.
atoms.negative_test_candidate: A canonical tip update should emit the `el_update_head` label with path `block_accept` and the new block hash/number/time.

summary: The block metrics module is exact-port preserved, including the block-build attempt trace model and ring buffer, attempt recording/snapshot APIs, generic phase logging, block verification lifecycle logs, payload-build lifecycle and phase logs, and execution-layer canonical head update logging.
